content.subtitle: Rule
items: With some time words and phrases, we use the present simple to talk about the future (not will / be going to).

content.subtitle: Common words
rows: I'll call you after we solve the problem.
I'll call you as soon as we solve the problem.
It'll be a few days before we find the solution.
I won't call you until we find the solution.
It'll be great when we find the solution.
I'll be in the office while I deal with this problem.

content.subtitle: Watch out
items: These time words and phrases can also go at the beginning of the sentence: As soon as we solve the problem, I'll call you.

content.subtitle: Use and form
items: We use although to express contrast.

content.subtitle: Examples
items: Although my homework was difficult, I finished it before bed.
I finished my homework before bed, although it was difficult.

content.subtitle: Use and form
items: We use in spite of and despite to express contrast. They mean the same thing.
in spite of / despite + -ing form, subject + verb
in spite of / despite + noun, subject + verb

content.subtitle: Examples
items: In spite of revising for hours, I didn't do well in the test.
Despite my revision, I didn't do well in the test.
I didn't do well in the test, despite revising for hours.

content.subtitle: Use and form
items: We use however to express contrast.

content.subtitle: Examples
items: We believed that we would find a solution. However, we were wrong.
We believed that we would find a solution. We were wrong, however.

content.subtitle: Meaning
items: unless means if ... not or except if.
Unless you hurry up, we'll be late. (= If you don't hurry up, we'll be late.)

sections.title: A: Time clauses
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with one time word or phrase.

items.sentence: I'll text you ...... I arrive at the station.
Don't leave ...... I finish explaining the task.
She checked the door ...... she went to bed.
We'll start dinner ...... Dad gets home.
Please wash your hands ...... you cook.
I usually listen to music ...... I do my homework.

sections.title: B: Although vs despite
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with although, despite or in spite of.

items.sentence: ...... it was raining, they continued playing football.
...... being tired, she finished the report.
He passed the exam ...... not studying much.
...... the traffic, we arrived on time.
...... I was nervous, I gave the presentation.
...... the noise, the baby stayed asleep.
He trained every day. ......, he didn't win the race.
We planned carefully. ......, the event had problems.
The task looked easy. It was difficult, ...... .
I wanted to buy it. ......, it was too expensive.
She studied a lot. She forgot some answers, ...... .
The hotel was comfortable. ......, the service was poor.
We won't catch the bus ...... we leave now.
You can't enter ...... you show your ID.
I'll call you tonight ...... I'm very late.
The plants will die ...... you water them.
She won't improve ...... she practises more.
I won't lend you my bike ...... you promise to be careful.
Call me as soon as you ...... home.
...... it was cold, they went for a walk.
Despite ...... hard, we couldn't finish on time.
She was ill. ......, she came to class.
We'll miss the start ...... we hurry.
I'll wait here ...... you finish.
